task_title: Audit unit test files (6 files, ~2490 lines)
false_positives_removed: unittest-001 — Explorer claimed assertion at line 155 is outside context block, but indentation shows it IS inside the 'with patch' block at same level as lines 150-154. Not a real issue.

findings.title: Assertion always passes: empty string is substring of any string
findings.problem: test_build_prompt_empty_context asserts 'context in prompt' where context=''. In Python, '' in any_string is always True. This test provides zero value — it never fails regardless of how empty context is handled.
findings.fix: Change assertion to verify the prompt does NOT contain context markers, or assert that the prompt correctly handles the absence of context (e.g., assert 'No relevant context' in prompt or assert context section is empty).
findings.evidence:
context = ""
...
assert context in prompt  # always True

findings.title: Overly permissive assertion: any response containing 'answer' passes
findings.problem: The assertion `assert 'answer' in result.answer.lower() or 'This is the answer' in result.answer` is extremely loose. Responses like 'I cannot answer this question', 'The answer is unclear', or any string containing 'answer' would pass. This test cannot detect regressions in the query pipeline.
findings.fix: Use the exact mocked response for assertion: assert result.answer == 'This is the answer based on context.'
findings.evidence: assert "answer" in result.answer.lower() or "This is the answer" in result.answer

findings.title: Unused fixture: inference_config never consumed by any test
findings.problem: The inference_config fixture is defined but never referenced by any test file. It imports llm_interface which may fail in isolated test environments. Unused fixtures add unnecessary import overhead.
findings.fix: Remove the unused fixture, or document its intended purpose with a comment.
findings.evidence: grep 'inference_config' in tests/ → only conftest.py definition

findings.title: Misleading test name: 'test_validate_url_valid' expects rejection
findings.problem: test_validate_url_valid at line 276 tests a localhost URL and expects ValueError. The name says 'valid' but the behavior tested is rejection. The comment on line 279 says 'localhost is rejected by the validation' which contradicts the test name.
findings.fix: Rename to test_validate_url_localhost_rejected or test_validate_url_rejects_localhost to accurately reflect the expected behavior.
findings.evidence:
def test_validate_url_valid(self):
    url = "http://localhost:11434"
    # localhost is rejected by the validation
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="URL must not point to localhost"):

findings.title: Floating point equality comparison is fragile
findings.problem: assert data[0]['similarity'] == 0.85 uses direct float equality. Floating point computations may introduce rounding errors causing intermittent test failures.
findings.fix: Use pytest.approx(): assert data[0]['similarity'] == pytest.approx(0.85, rel=1e-2)

findings.title: RRF fusion test uses weak tied assertions
findings.problem: The RRF fusion test uses 'in' operator to check if results are tied: assert fused[0][0] in [0, 1]. This assertion passes for both correct and incorrect implementations as long as one of the top results has index 0 or 1.
findings.fix: Add stronger assertions verifying exact ranking based on RRF formula, or use test data where ties cannot occur.

findings.title: BM25 tests have conditional assertions based on optional dependency
findings.problem: Tests like test_bm25_index_build and test_bm25_index_search behave differently when rank_bm25 is not installed. Tests skip silently with weaker assertions, reducing coverage without clear documentation.
findings.fix: Add explicit pytest.mark.skipif for BM25 tests when rank_bm25 is not available, or mock rank_bm25 for consistent behavior.

findings.title: Over-mocking external libraries: tests cannot detect real integration issues
findings.problem: PDF and DOCX extraction tests completely mock pdfplumber.open and docx.Document. These tests verify the test author's mock expectations, not whether the code correctly calls the real libraries. Real regressions in library usage would pass undetected.
findings.fix: Add at least one integration test per extraction type using actual library calls (marked with @pytest.mark.integration). Keep mocked tests for CI speed but add real tests for confidence.

findings.title: Mid-sentence split detection uses unreliable heuristic
findings.problem: The sentence boundary preservation test uses chunk_text[-1].islower() and chunk_text[-2] == ' ' as a heuristic. This may produce false positives (valid sentences ending with lowercase words) or false negatives (mid-sentence splits that happen to end with uppercase).
findings.fix: Verify actual sentence boundaries using the same tokenizer logic as document_processor, or use a more robust approach.

findings.title: Conditional assertion allows test to pass with empty results
findings.problem: test_window_expansion_with_chunks uses 'if chunks:' before asserting, meaning the test passes silently when no chunks are returned. This hides failures in the expansion logic.
findings.fix: Add assert len(chunks) > 0 before the conditional block to fail the test when no data is retrieved.

findings.title: Overlap test uses word count comparison instead of verifying actual overlap
findings.problem: test_chunking_overlap verifies overlap by checking len(all_words) >= len(set(all_words)). This is an indirect proxy — it would pass for any text with repeated words, not just overlapping chunks.
findings.fix: Verify specific overlapping content: check that the last N words of chunk[i] appear at the start of chunk[i+1].

findings.title: Direct mutation of shared fixture state may affect other tests
findings.problem: test_hybrid_search_falls_back_to_vector directly sets vector_store.bm25_index = None. If the vector_store fixture is shared across tests, this mutation could affect subsequent tests.
findings.fix: Use monkeypatch fixture to temporarily modify the attribute with automatic cleanup.

findings.title: Try-except around test body masks real failures as ImportError
findings.problem: test_gguf_valid_magic_bytes wraps the test body in try/except ImportError. If GGUF backend has a real bug, the test silently skips instead of failing.
findings.fix: Use pytest.importorskip('llama_cpp') at module level to make the skip explicit and separate from test logic.

findings.title: Test expects ConnectionError but implementation may raise different exception
findings.problem: test_ollama_connection_error expects ConnectionError with specific message. The OllamaLLM implementation may raise urllib.error.URLError or other exception types that wouldn't match.
findings.fix: Verify the actual exception type raised by OllamaLLM._verify_connection() and use the correct type.

findings.title: Module-level TestClient instantiation may cause side effects
findings.problem: client = TestClient(app) at module level (line 23) creates the FastAPI test client at import time, triggering app initialization. This may cause side effects or make test isolation harder.
findings.fix: Move TestClient creation into a pytest fixture for proper lifecycle management.

findings.title: vector_store fixture accepts unused mock_llm parameter
findings.problem: The vector_store fixture lists mock_llm as a parameter but never references it in the fixture body. This is misleading about the fixture's dependencies.
findings.fix: Remove mock_llm from the parameter list, or add a comment explaining why it's needed for side effects.
